title: What is the Daily Office?
summary: The ancient Christian practice of praying at fixed hours throughout the day.

title: What is Ordinary Time?
summary: The longest season of the church year — a time for steady growth in faith.

title: What is Lent?
summary: Forty days of preparation, penitence, and renewal before Easter.

title: What is Advent?
summary: A season of expectation and preparation for both Christmas and Christ's return.

title: What is Epiphany?
summary: The revelation of Christ to the world — light shining in darkness.

title: What is Pentecost?
summary: The coming of the Holy Spirit and the birth of the Church.

title: Why Liturgical Seasons?
summary: How the church year shapes our spiritual lives by walking through the story of salvation.

title: Why Do We Use Collects?
summary: These ancient, carefully crafted prayers "collect" our intentions before God.

title: What is the Venite?
summary: Psalm 95 — the ancient call to worship that opens Morning Prayer.

title: What is the Benedictus?
summary: Zechariah's song of praise at the birth of John the Baptist.

title: What is the Nunc Dimittis?
summary: Simeon's song — the ancient night prayer of release and peace.

title: What is the Invitatory?
summary: The opening dialogue that calls us from our daily lives into the presence of God.

title: What is the Gloria Patri?
summary: The brief doxology affirming the Trinity, prayed since the fourth century.

title: What is the Apostles' Creed?
summary: The oldest summary of Christian faith — what the Church has always believed.

title: Why Confession of Sin?
summary: Beginning prayer with honest acknowledgment clears the way for genuine encounter with God.

title: What is the Psalter?
summary: The prayer book of Jesus and the early church — 150 psalms for every human emotion.
